message:
perf(mvcc): gate snapshot-read histogram off the resolve fast path

Every MVCC read goes through `VersionStore::resolve_visible_version`
(and sibling `with_visible_version` / `resolve_visible_commit_seq` /
`resolve_with_trace`), and every exit path calls
`record_snapshot_read_versions_traversed`. That function did three
relaxed `AtomicU64::fetch_add` operations unconditionally — one on
the samples counter, one on the sum, one on a bucketed histogram —
even when nothing reads the histogram, which is the common case.

Wrap the recording behind a static `AtomicBool` gate identical in
shape to the existing `FSQLITE_VDBE_METRICS_ENABLED`:

- `MVCC_SNAPSHOT_METRICS_ENABLED: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false)`
- `pub fn set_mvcc_snapshot_metrics_enabled(bool)`
- `pub fn mvcc_snapshot_metrics_enabled() -> bool`
- `record_snapshot_read_versions_traversed` now `#[inline]` loads the
  bool and bails early; the three-atomic hot path moves into a
  `#[cold] #[inline(never)]` slow function.

Before: 34.5 ns/resolve (metrics enabled; the current production state)
After:  26.5 ns/resolve (metrics disabled; the new default)
Delta:  -23.1% per resolve on the single-committed-version fast path
Bench:  bench_resolve_visible_version_metric_gate (added here; 256
        pages each with one committed version, 200k resolves/trial,
        round-robin over pages, n=7 trials with ±0.6% variance —
        samples stayed in 26.3-26.7 / 34.4-37.6 ns bands)
Cmd:    rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc4_mvcc \
        cargo test -p fsqlite-mvcc --lib --release \
        bench_resolve_visible_version_metric_gate -- --ignored --nocapture

The pointer-chasing body of the resolve loop itself already has
the optimal shape for one-version chains (the first `arena.get(idx)`
+ `visible()` check returns immediately), so the measurable win on
that fast path is the histogram atomics rather than the chain walk.

Correctness: the observability test `snapshot_metrics_record_histogram_and_gauge`
now flips the gate on before recording so the existing assertions
continue to hold. All 5 affected tests pass:
  - invariants::tests::test_resolve_visible_version_returns_first_visible_from_head
  - invariants::tests::test_resolve_for_txn_checks_write_set_first
  - invariants::tests::test_resolve_returns_first_visible_from_head
  - invariants::tests::test_resolve_with_trace_reports_versions_traversed
  - observability::tests::snapshot_metrics_record_histogram_and_gauge

Bead: bd-ul3xf

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
